linear_9.label: Linear Equations & Inequalities
misconceptions: Dividing by a negative number doesn't change inequality direction
All equations have exactly one solution
Literal equations are different from standard equations
linear_9.advanced: Solves complex equations with rational coefficients and variables on both sides, including cases with no solution or infinitely many solutions. Writes and solves literal equations and compound inequalities. Creates equations from verbal descriptions and justifies solution methods algebraically.
linear_9.proficient: Solves multi-step linear equations and inequalities with integer and simple rational coefficients. Writes equations from real-world contexts. Graphs solution sets on a number line and verifies solutions by substitution.
linear_9.developing: Solves one- and two-step linear equations with integer coefficients. Solves simple inequalities but may struggle with multi-step or those requiring division by negative numbers. Graphs simple inequalities on a number line with support.
linear_9.beginning: Identifies linear equations and inequalities but cannot consistently solve them. Recognizes addition/subtraction steps but struggles with multiplication/division. Needs significant step-by-step guidance.

systems_9.label: Systems of Linear Equations
misconceptions: Systems always have exactly one solution
Graphing is the only accurate method
A solution must have integer coordinates
systems_9.advanced: Solves systems using multiple methods (substitution, elimination, graphing) and selects the most efficient approach. Identifies and explains systems with one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions. Creates systems from real-world scenarios and interprets solutions in context.
systems_9.proficient: Solves systems algebraically using substitution or elimination. Solves by graphing when equations are in slope-intercept form. Verifies solutions and identifies when a system is inconsistent or dependent.
systems_9.developing: Solves simple systems using substitution when one equation is already solved for a variable. Solves by graphing with support. Identifies the intersection point as the solution.
systems_9.beginning: Recognizes that systems have solutions but cannot solve them reliably. Graphs individual lines but struggles to find the intersection. Needs step-by-step guidance for both algebraic and graphical methods.

quadratic_9.label: Quadratic Expressions & Equations
misconceptions: All quadratic equations can be factored over the integers
The quadratic formula doesn't work for some quadratics
Complex solutions mean the equation has no solutions
quadratic_9.advanced: Solves quadratic equations using all three methods (factoring, completing the square, quadratic formula) and explains when each is most efficient. Identifies solutions as real or complex. Applies quadratics to model real-world situations and interprets solutions in context.
quadratic_9.proficient: Solves quadratic equations by factoring and using the quadratic formula. Solves by completing the square for simple cases. Determines the nature of solutions using the discriminant and graphs parabolas.
quadratic_9.developing: Solves simple quadratics by factoring or when in perfect square form. Applies the quadratic formula with support. May struggle with equations that don't factor nicely.
quadratic_9.beginning: Recognizes quadratic equations but cannot solve them reliably. Can factor simple expressions with guidance. Does not yet independently select or execute solution methods.

polynomial_9.label: Polynomials & Factoring
misconceptions: FOIL is the only way to multiply binomials
All quadratic trinomials can be factored
polynomial_9.advanced: Factors polynomials using all techniques (GCF, grouping, difference of squares, trinomials) and applies factoring to solve real-world problems. Multiplies polynomials efficiently using area models or distribution. Explains why certain polynomials cannot be factored over the integers.
polynomial_9.proficient: Adds, subtracts, and multiplies polynomials accurately. Factors out GCF and factors trinomials and difference of squares. Uses factored forms to solve equations.
polynomial_9.developing: Adds and subtracts polynomials correctly. Multiplies polynomials using FOIL or distribution with support. Factors simple expressions with GCF and some trinomials.
polynomial_9.beginning: Identifies polynomials and like terms. Performs basic addition and subtraction with guidance. Cannot reliably multiply or factor independently.

function_9.label: Functions & Relations
misconceptions: The vertical line test is optional
Domain and range are always the same
function_9.advanced: Analyzes functions represented in multiple forms and determines domain and range from equations and graphs. Compares different functions to identify growth patterns. Describes qualitatively how functions change (increasing/decreasing, rate of change) and creates functions to model real-world relationships.
function_9.proficient: Evaluates functions using function notation. Determines if relations are functions using the vertical line test or ordered pairs. Finds domain and range from equations and graphs. Identifies linear functions and calculates rate of change.
function_9.developing: Evaluates simple functions when provided equations. Identifies functions in tables and mapping diagrams. Struggles to find domain and range from equations. Determines rate of change from graphs with support.
function_9.beginning: Understands the concept of input and output. Recognizes functions in simple contexts. Cannot yet identify functions reliably or find domain and range independently.
